new_classes.label_en: Other vinegar (not wine vinegar), in containers holding ≤ 2 l (CN 2209 00 91)
new_classes.label_de: Anderer Essig (kein Weinessig), in Behältnissen mit einem Inhalt von ≤ 2 l (KN 2209 00 91)
new_classes.definition_en: Vinegar, fermented vinegar and substitutes for vinegar obtained from acetic acid, other than wine vinegar, in containers holding 2 litres or less. Includes fermented vinegars obtained from other sources (e.g. cider, malt, spirit, grain, rice) and substitutes for vinegar obtained from acetic acid, as described in HS Explanatory Note to heading 2209, part (I), second paragraph (2), (3) and (4), and part (II).

Quality borne by a vinegar product indicating that it is not wine vinegar; it covers fermented vinegars from sources other than wine (e.g. cider vinegar, malt vinegar, spirit vinegar, grain vinegar, rice vinegar) and substitutes for vinegar obtained from acetic acid, as listed in HS Explanatory Note to heading 2209, part (I), second paragraph (2)–(4) and part (II).

coverage_explanation:
The three core discriminating criteria are captured: (1) the product is vinegar / vinegar substitute under CN 2209 (via parent class Vinegar2209), (2) it is NOT wine vinegar (complement restriction on WineVinegarQuality, aligned with sibling subheadings 22090011 and 22090019), and (3) it is in containers holding ≤ 2 l (SmallContainer part restriction). The sibling code 22090099 covers the same product type in containers > 2 l.

What is not fully captured: the legal note references HS Explanatory Note to heading 2209, part (I) second paragraph (2)–(4) and part (II), which enumerates specific sub-types (cider vinegar, malt vinegar, spirit vinegar, rice vinegar, and acetic acid substitutes). These product-type distinctions are bundled into the single quality OtherVinegarQuality rather than modelled as separate named qualities, because no further CN subheading split is made among these sub-types at this level. The coverage score is therefore 0.8 rather than 1.0 to reflect that the internal enumeration from the HS Explanatory Notes is not individually axiomatised.
